title: Tapping for bold action without fear
subtitle: A tapping practice for when overthinking delays the next right move.
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 44 of the MONEY program. The script works with the pattern of over-assessing before acting — staying in analysis until the window closes.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 44 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025).

practice.whenToUse: Use before a decision point you've been circling — a pitch, a price, a conversation you've been putting off.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 44 of the MONEY program. The script targets the pattern of replaying possible mistakes until the moment for action has passed.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I sometimes hesitate when action is needed, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I fear making the wrong move, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've held back on bold steps, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round (repeat x3)
**Eyebrow:** I can overthink the next move.
**Side of eye:** I worry about what might go wrong.
**Under eye:** I replay possible mistakes.
**Under nose:** I delay until I feel certain.
**Chin:** I sometimes miss the window.
**Collarbone:** I'm tired of freezing when it matters.
**Under arm:** I want to trust my decisions.
**Top of head:** I'm ready to act boldly.
Reframe to positive (repeat x3)
**Eyebrow:** I trust myself to take smart, fast action.
**Side of eye:** I move with confidence when ideas feel right.
**Under eye:** I know every step teaches and builds wealth.
**Under nose:** I act even when the path isn't perfect.
**Chin:** I stay calm and decisive.
**Collarbone:** I love the freedom of bold action.
**Under arm:** I create momentum by trusting my gut.
**Top of head:** I act boldly and let abundance follow.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from
Adapted from Day 44 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). EFT tapping developed by Gary Craig in the 1990s.
